name: Candy Bar Torte
recipeIngredient: 3 creamy white candy bars with almonds (2.2-ounce bars)
recipeInstructions:
Reserve half of one candy bar for garnish. Break up the remaining bars. Heat and stir the broken candy and water in a small saucepan over low heat until melted. Cool for 20 minutes. Grease and flour three 9-inch round cake pans.
Combine the sifted flour, baking powder, and baking soda; set aside.
Beat butter or margarine in a large mixing bowl with an electric mixer on medium speed for 30 seconds. Add the 1 1/2 cups sugar; beat until fluffy. Beat in egg yolks; beat in melted candy.
Add the flour mixture and buttermilk alternately to the candy mixture, beating after each addition. Wash beaters thoroughly.
Beat egg whites in another bowl until soft peaks form (tips curl). Gradually beat in the 1/4 cup sugar; beat until stiff peaks form (tips stand straight).
Fold the beaten egg whites into the batter. Divide the batter evenly among the prepared pans.
Bake at 350°F for 30 minutes or until a wooden toothpick inserted in centers comes out clean. Cool in the pans on wire racks for 10 minutes; remove the cakes from the pans. Cool.
To serve, spread jam between layers; frost the top and sides of the assembled cake with Whipped Cream Frosting. Chop the reserved candy; sprinkle on top of the cake.
Makes 12 servings
Whipped Cream Frosting: Beat 1 1/2 cups whipping cream with 1/4 cup sifted powdered sugar and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla until soft peaks form (tips curl).
